summary.intent: The user wanted to simplify the build process by committing the pre-built Tailwind CSS artifact so that `cargo build` works without Node.js dependencies.
summary.outcome: Removed `style.css` from `.gitignore`, committed the built CSS file, and updated `CLAUDE.md` to include a reminder to rebuild and commit the CSS when adding new Tailwind classes.

repo: The project uses `just build-css` (Tailwind) as a separate build step from `cargo build`, creating a two-step build dependency for fresh clones.
CI has a dedicated 'Build Tailwind CSS' step that would catch stale committed CSS artifacts.
CLAUDE.md already documented the Tailwind rebuild requirement; it was extended to include committing the artifact.
sessions.spec.js tests accumulate sessions across the test run, and RPC calls like `clear_all` that iterate all sessions become prohibitively slow at scale (299 sequential tests with 1 worker).
channels-nostr.spec.js uses a 5s visibility timeout that is tighter than the project default of 10s, causing flakiness in CI.

code.finding: The `sessions.clear_all` RPC call was removed because it timed out due to accumulated sessions from prior tests; the test uses a unique `data-session-key` selector so leftover sessions don't affect correctness.
Visibility timeout for `getByText('my-nostr-bot')` bumped from 5s to 10s to match the project default and reduce CI flakiness.

workflow: Committing build artifacts is a valid tradeoff when the artifact is small, rarely changes, and removing the build step lowers the barrier for contributors.
When a test already uses a unique selector, removing a global cleanup call is safe and avoids timeout issues at scale.

friction: `cargo build` failed on fresh clones without Node.js/Tailwind installed because `style.css` was gitignored.
CI test flakiness in both `sessions.spec.js` (timeout from session accumulation) and `channels-nostr.spec.js` (tight visibility timeout) required investigation and fixes before the session.
open_items: CSS PRs must now remember to run `just build-css` and commit the updated `style.css` — this is documented in CLAUDE.md but relies on contributor discipline.
The root cause of session accumulation slowness in `sessions.spec.js` (heavyweight per-session cleanup) was worked around but not addressed structurally.
